title: Bulk report - Week 31
summary:
Capesize
A quiet end to the week could not disguise the improved sentiment in the capesize market, with several brokers suggesting that this may simply represent a precursor to another move upwards. The timecharter average climbed $1,282 to close at $18,296, driven predominantly by the Brazil and Australian round voyages on continued iron ore demand strength. The C3 Brazil-China route gained $1.53 to close at $17.47, whilst the C5 Australia-China route posted $1.16 to close at $7.155 on the week.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A quiet end to the week could not disguise the improved sentiment in the capesize market, with several brokers suggesting that this may simply represent a precursor to another move upwards. The timecharter average climbed $1,282 to close at $18,296, driven predominantly by the Brazil and Australian round voyages on continued iron ore demand strength.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The panamax market proved to be quite a contrasting week, with rates globally for the early part still severely under pressure. By midweek, sentiment and fresh enquiry - noticeably from EC South America - had improved.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A poor week overall for the BSI with the index falling 36 points from Monday. Little in the way of period activity was reported with Owners reluctant to commit.

children.title: Handy
children.summary: The overall index BHSI continued climbing since early May and remained at the highest level of the year this week. However, it also showed a minor decline for the first time in the last three months.
